snapshot: At 11:00 JST the Nikkei was 51,921.73, up 35.88, after reversing from an early drop of more than 1,300 points.
Prime-market breadth was 1,181 gainers, 352 losers and 37 unchanged, so the internal tape is stronger than the headline index move suggests.
USD/JPY stayed in the upper 159s. The 160 level and intervention risk are still close, but the pair did not break through convincingly in the morning.
The Nikkei 225 night-session futures close was 51,100. Cash equities traded back above that overnight level as oil pressure temporarily eased.
Still, the rebound was triggered mainly by reports that Trump may be willing to end operations, so the afternoon remains vulnerable to renewed geopolitical volatility.

drivers.driver: An early washout turned into a mechanical rebound
drivers.why_today: The market was down more than 1,300 points shortly after the open, but the Nikkei recovered back into positive territory as oil stopped rising. The key afternoon question is whether that rebound can hold.

drivers.driver: Some easing in the oil shock, but not a clean de-escalation
drivers.why_today: Reports that Trump may be willing to end the operation helped cool oil intraday, while USD/JPY stayed in the upper 159s. The worst panic eased, but the setup is still fragile.

drivers.driver: Only clear stock-specific catalysts are outperforming
drivers.why_today: Zojirushi, KADOKAWA, Tekscend and Shimamura are leading because their stories are simple to explain: earnings, activist buying, new coverage and guidance with dividend support.

headline_inventory.headline: The Nikkei 225 night-session futures close was 51,100
Reuters' oil story had Brent at $115.04 and WTI at $105.96 early in the session
USD/JPY stayed in the upper 159s, with intervention risk still limiting any clean move through 160
The Nikkei turned positive at 10:35 JST after oil gains paused on the Trump report
At 11:00 JST the Nikkei was 51,921.73 (+35.88) and Prime-market breadth stood at 1,181 gainers versus 352 losers
Zojirushi Mahobin became the top Prime-market gainer after its strong quarterly result
KADOKAWA rose after Oasis increased its stake to 13.76%
Zojirushi Mahobin reported a 23% increase in first-quarter ordinary profit
Shimamura posted another record-profit plan and signaled a real dividend increase for FY ending February 2027
UACJ and Nippon Light Metal were bid on higher aluminium-price expectations after the Iran smelter attack

watchlist.name: Zojirushi Corporation
watchlist.why_today: A 28% increase in Q1 operating profit drove an 11.2% intraday jump, making it the cleanest earnings reaction in the Prime market this morning.
watchpoints: Can it hold around the 1,650-yen area and stay near the highs
Does the market start pricing upside to the full-year plan despite no formal raise
Can real dip-buying volume continue into the afternoon
falsifiers: The stock starts filling the gap lower after the opening surge
Investors treat the strong Q1 only as a good start without changing the full-year view
watchlist.red_team: The quarter was strong, but without a full-year raise the initial burst can still cool quickly.

sources.title: ホットストック：象印マホービンが値上がり率トップ、好決算を材料視

watchlist.name: KADOKAWA
watchlist.why_today: Oasis raised its holding to 13.76%. The stock is reacting as the market starts extending the capital-action and proposal angle.
watchpoints: Can it keep trading around the 3,800-yen area
Can it stay positive into the afternoon
Does the market keep pricing future proposal potential rather than just the filing itself
falsifiers: The move loses momentum once the filing surprise is fully digested
The market decides the stake is not enough to change the story near term
watchlist.red_team: Activist headlines are powerful, but without a visible next step the move can remain mostly positional.

watchlist.name: Tekscend Photomask
watchlist.why_today: Nomura initiated coverage with a Buy rating, and the stock is standing out as a rare semi-related winner in a choppy tape.
watchpoints: Can it keep the 2,900-yen area and stay near the highs
Does turnover stay active enough for a real follow-through move
Can the new-coverage story stay linked to the recent earnings upgrade narrative
falsifiers: The move fades as just a one-day broker note reaction
Broader semiconductor weakness pulls it back
watchlist.red_team: Brokerage-coverage catalysts often work quickly, but they also fade quickly when the tape weakens.

watchlist.name: Shimamura
watchlist.why_today: The company guided to another record year and a real dividend increase. It recovered from early weakness and is holding in positive territory.
watchpoints: Can it keep trading around the 3,300-yen area
Does the higher dividend support a defensive-retail bid
Can the stock hold its gain even if the wider market softens again
falsifiers: The market decides the shareholder-return angle is not strong enough
The stock slips back into negative territory after the morning rebound
watchlist.red_team: The guidance is solid, but the surprise factor is not extreme enough to guarantee a trend move.

watchlist.name: UACJ
watchlist.why_today: The stock is being bid on higher aluminium-price expectations after the Iran smelter attack, making it a clean second-order geopolitical theme.
watchpoints: Can it hold the morning gain into the afternoon
Does the aluminium-price theme keep drawing buyers
Can it outperform Nippon Light Metal on a relative basis
falsifiers: The theme fades quickly after the initial headline response
Any easing in Middle East tension knocks back the aluminium narrative
watchlist.red_team: This is still a theme-driven move, so the reversal can be fast if the narrative cools.

watchlist.name: Retty
watchlist.why_today: A securities-sale gain drove a 3.9x increase in the net-profit outlook, and the stock is still holding a positive reaction in the morning session.
watchpoints: Can it keep the 150-yen area into the afternoon
Does the market keep rewarding the higher net-profit view
Can interest in smaller catalyst-driven names stay alive
falsifiers: The move is dismissed as only a special-gain story
Thin turnover turns it into a fade from the morning high
watchlist.red_team: Because the revision is driven by a special gain, investors can question the quality of the uplift quickly.

deep_dive.title: Zojirushi: the key afternoon test is whether a clean quarterly beat can stay bid after the early surge

lines: This looks less like a pure short-covering move and more like a clean earnings reaction built on price pass-through and stronger domestic sales.
Among large-cap consumer names, Zojirushi offered the clearest first-quarter surprise and the cleanest price response in the morning session.
After the March 30 close, the company reported a 23% increase in first-quarter ordinary profit and a 28% increase in operating profit, with a strong progress rate against the full-year plan.
Reuters confirmed that by 09:48 JST on March 31 the stock was up more than 10% and was the top Prime-market gainer.
Can it hold around the 1,650-yen area instead of simply fading after the opening gap.
Even without a formal full-year raise, does the market keep pricing upside and support the stock with real dip-buying volume.
The stock starts filling the gap lower after the opening surge.
Investors decide that the first-quarter progress is strong but not enough to change the full-year case.
The quarter was strong, but without a full-year raise the initial burst can still cool quickly.
Consumer-electronics names are not fully defensive if the broader market mood deteriorates again.

no_trade_conditions: If the Nikkei gives back most of the 11:00 JST rebound and turns decisively lower again
If crude spikes again and reverses the afternoon risk tone
If USD/JPY jumps back toward 160 or intervention-style volatility returns suddenly
If the top catalyst names such as Zojirushi and KADOKAWA fail to hold their morning high zones and break lower on real volume
